data.title: Thailand International Digital Business and Financial Center Appoints Movement Co-founder and Two Other Crypto Industry Professionals as Advisors
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Wai Man Raymond Chu, director of the Thailand International Digital Business and Financial Center (TIDC), announced the appointment of crypto industry figures Tekin Salimi and Rushi Manche as advisors to the center. This strategic move highlights TIDC's commitment to developing the country's blockchain and stablecoin infrastructure, promoting cryptocurrency adoption, and building a team to drive Thailand's digital economy. The addition of these two experts is expected to provide guidance and support for the center's initiatives.
TIDC is an important collaboration supported by Thailand's Ministry of Digital Economy and Society (MDES) and National Telecommunications Public Company Limited (NT) to cultivate a digital-first ecosystem, attract digital business investment, and promote the development of digital innovation in Thailand.
According to reports, Tekin Salimi is an entrepreneur, venture capitalist, and former lawyer who founded the cryptocurrency asset management fund dao5, which manages more than $500 million in assets.
Rushi Manche is the co-founder of Movement Labs, a blockchain development company dedicated to improving the security and efficiency of blockchain networks.

data.title: Thailand's TIDC Appoints New Advisors to Boost Digital Economy
data.description:
According to Odaily, the Thailand International Digital Business and Financial Center (TIDC) has appointed Tekin Salimi and Rushi Manche as advisors. This strategic move underscores TIDC's commitment to enhancing the country's blockchain and stablecoin infrastructure, promoting cryptocurrency adoption, and building a team to advance Thailand's digital economy. The expertise of these two professionals is expected to guide and support the center's initiatives.

TIDC is a significant collaboration supported by Thailand's Ministry of Digital Economy and Society (MDES) and National Telecom Public Company Limited (NT). It aims to foster a digital-first ecosystem, attract digital business investments, and drive digital innovation in Thailand.

Tekin Salimi is an entrepreneur, venture capitalist, and former lawyer who founded the cryptocurrency asset management fund dao5, which manages assets exceeding $500 million. Rushi Manche is the co-founder of Movement Labs, a blockchain development company focused on enhancing the security and efficiency of blockchain networks.

data.title: Bitfinex: Bitcoin's trend may change decisively in the next few weeks
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Bitfinex analysts said in a recent market report that Bitcoin's trend may change decisively, "especially as more macroeconomic developments unfold." They pointed out that Bitcoin has been trading in a 15% price range since hovering around $90,000 in mid-November last year. Historically, the 15-20% comprehensive price range tends to "break out in either direction within 80-90 days." It said that although Bitcoin is "highly correlated" with macro conditions and US President Trump's tariffs on Canada, Mexico and China have caused turmoil in the cryptocurrency market, Bitcoin has recently been able to maintain a price above about $70,000 before the US election, which shows the relative strength of the asset. Trump's tariff policy led to the "largest liquidation event in cryptocurrency history" on February 3, with more than $2.24 billion in cryptocurrency markets exploded within 24 hours. Analysts added that "although Bitcoin's short-term volatility may continue due to macroeconomic impacts, its long-term prospects remain optimistic."

data.title: Bitcoin chart signals ‘decisive price move’ in coming weeks: Analyst
data.description:
Bitcoin may see a “decisive price move” in the near term, and while short-term volatility may continue, the long-term view “remains positive,” say analysts.
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/bitcoin-chart-signals-decisive-price-move-coming-macro-economic-conditions-analysts?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: Trump Administration Agrees to Temporarily Limit DOGE’s Access to Treasury Payment Systems
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News - U.S. Department of Justice prosecutors have agreed to temporarily restrict Musk's "Department of Government Efficiency" (DOGE) staff from accessing information in the Treasury Department's payment system. 
Earlier, a group of union members and retirees sued the Treasury Department, alleging that providing DOGE with access to the federal government's vast payment system and personal data information stored therein violated federal privacy laws. 
The Trump administration filed a motion on Wednesday night seeking to sign a proposed order detailing the agreed terms. The proposed order states that "the defendant will not provide any access to payment records or payment systems maintained by the U.S. Fiscal Service or its internal agencies." The order allows exceptions, allowing two special government employees within the Treasury Department (Tom Kraus and Marco Elez) to access these records "on demand" when performing their duties, but this access is limited to "read-only". (Jin Shi)

data.title: Flow Traders, QCP, and GSR deposited a total of 57.62 million stablecoins to CEX in the past 24 hours
Brazilian lawmakers propose new draft bill to allow Brazilian funds to invest in cryptocurrencies
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Brazilian Congressman Adriana Ventura submitted a new draft bill proposing to approve Brazilian funds to invest in cryptocurrencies and purchase Bitcoin. The bill has been uploaded to the official website of the Brazilian Congress.
Ventura said that the proposal is aimed at meeting the growing demand and interest of Brazilian investment funds in cryptocurrencies. "This measure may contribute to financial innovation, encourage the industry to develop more complex products, and formulate investment strategies that include crypto assets. It will also encourage internal competition and promote creativity in the financial sector."
She added that the measure will "provide a regulated and safe environment," which will enable funds to expand the diversification of their portfolios.
